rulings.Case Title: MARSAE SCOTT et al VS. LENNAR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Deeming Req.'S For Admissions Admitted, Compelling Pltf'S Responses To Interrogatories And Req. For Production Of Documents And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 9, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CH2M HILL's Motion For An Order Deeming Requests For Admissions Admitted, Compelling Plaintiff's Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions. Granted. Within 10 days of notice of this order, plaintiffs to provide documents or statements confirming no responsive documents after a reasonable and diligent search. All supplemental responses must be properly verified. As to those plaintiffs who have not responded to requests for admissions, the admissions are deemed admitted. All PLAINTIFFS AND plaintiffs' counsel must pay defendant reasonable sanctions in the amount of $ 1,619.50. C.C.P. Sections 2023.020, 2023.030 and 2033.280. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EDITH KLIMAN VS. ELISA STEPHENS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defts To Allow Inspection Of All Elevators At Defts' Property
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 9, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF EDITH KLIMAN'S Motion To Compel Defendants To Allow Inspection Of All Elevators At Defendants' Property. GRANTED. THE DISCOVERY SOUGHT IS RELEVANT AND APPEARS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. WHETHER SUCH EVIDENCE IS ULTIMATELY ADMITTED IS A MATTER TO BE DETERMINED AT TRIAL. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MENG LI VS. MACY'S INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ALSO KNOWN AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Privilege Log, And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 9, 2010, line 2, PLAINTIFF MENG LI's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Privilege Log, And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions. Granted. Macy's West Stores, Inc. is ordered to provide supplemental responses and a privilege log within 30 days of notice of this order. Reasonable sanctions in the amount of $4000 shall be paid by Macy's West Stores, Inc. to plaintiff. C.C.P. Sections 2030.300 and 2031.310. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. GREGORY YOCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prefiling Order For Vexatious Litigant And To Require Security; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 9, 2010, line 12, DEFENDANTS GREGORY YOCH, JAMES DEVINCENTI, MARCUS & MILLICHAP REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT'S Motion For Prefiling Order For Vexatious Litigant And To Require Security. GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN THAT AT LEAST 5 CASES WERE DETERMINED AGAINST ENG IN THE PAST 7 YEARS. DEFENDANTS ALSO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S CASE LACKS MERIT, AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THE INSTANT ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $100,000 WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING PROOF THAT SUCH SECURITY HAS BEEN FURNISHED. IF THE BOND IS NOT TIMELY POSTED, DEFENDANTS MAY SEEK DISMISSAL ON THE EX PARTE CALENDAR. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO CHRONICLE VS. KAMALA HARRIS, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate Under The California Public Records Act
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on 11/9/10, line 8, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO CHRONICLE'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate Under The California Public Records Act. DENIED. THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT ("CPRA") SERVES A VITAL PUBLIC PURPOSE, GROUNDED IN THE RECOGNITION THAT ACCESS TO INFORMATION CONCERNING THE CONDUCT OF THE PEOPLE'S BUSINESS IS A FUNDAMENTAL AND NECESSARY RIGHT OF EVERY PERSON IN THIS STATE. HOWEVER, THE CPRA ALSO RECOGNIZES THAT CERTAIN RECORDS SHOULD NOT, FOR THE REASONS OF PRIVACY, SAFETY, AND EFFICIENT GOVERNMENTAL OPERATION BE MADE PUBLIC. HAYNIE V. SUPERIOR COURT (2001) 26 CAL.4TH 1061. PETITIONER MADE TWO REQUESTS TO THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S OFFICE, EXHIBITS A AND B TO THE PETITION. THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY DEMONSTRATES THAT THE RECORDS IN QUESTION ARE EXEMPT, OR OTHERWISE DO NOT EXIST. (THE DELIBERATIVE PROCESS PRIVILEGE EXISTS, AND SURVIVES THE ENACTMENT OF PROPOSITION 59. PROPOSITION 59 DID NOT SPECIFICALLY REFERENCE THE DELIBERATIVE PROCESS PRIVILEGE. IT DID NOT ALTER THE SCOPE OF THE PUBLIC AGENCIES' DISCLOSURE OBLIGATIONS UNDER THE CPRA. IT SIMPLY "CONSTITUTIONALIZED" THE PRINCIPLES ALREADY SET FORTH IN THE CPRA AND DID NOT CHANGE EXISTING LAW. SUTTER'S PLACE V. SUPERIOR COURT (2008) 161 CAL.APP.4TH 1370. THE DELIBERATIVE PROCESS PRIVILEGE PROTECTS THE CONFIDENTIALITY OF COMMUNICATIONS TO AND AMONG AGENCY DECISION MAKERS, PARTICULARLY PRE-DECISIONAL COMMUNICATIONS RELATING TO THE FORMATION OF POLICY, ENCOURAGING CANDID DISCUSSION WITHOUT CONCERN THAT TENTATIVE BUT REJECTED THOUGHTS WILL BECOME SUBJECT OF PUBLIC DISCUSSION. SEE TIMES MIRROR CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1991) 53 CAL.3D 1325. THE HENDERSON DECLARATION SUFFICIENTLY SUPPORTS THE EXEMPTION AND NONDISCLOSURE AS THE BROADLY WORDED EXHIBIT A APPLIES TO DA-SFPD COMMUNICATIONS CONCERNING THE FORMATION OF AN "EXTERNAL BRADY" POLICY. RESPONDENT HAS PROPERLY DECLINED TO SEARCH OR DISCLOSE RECORDS FROM LAW ENFORCEMENT INVESTIGATORY FILES. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 6254(f). RESPONDENT NEED NOT DISCLOSE POLICE OFF
